headline: AI Shifts from Chatbot to Coworker—Execs Scramble on Scaling & ROI

stories.title: AI Systems Move from Tools to Autonomous Co-Workers
stories.summary: Ethan Mollick says companies need to stop treating AI as advanced chatbots and start recognizing autonomous agents that can carry out hours of independent work reshaping strategy, coding, and risk assessment.
stories.takeaway: The real game change isn't AI answering questions—it's AI systems doing actual work independently fr. Companies sleeping on this are gonna regret it.

stories.title: Big Four Spent $400B on AI Last Year; 2026 Budget Hits $650B
stories.summary: Benedict Evans breaks down massive capex commitments from major tech platforms and why the infrastructure crunch is real—from GPU shortages to electricity constraints.
stories.takeaway: The money flowing into AI is lowkey insane, but here's the thing: building enough capacity to actually deploy it all is the real bottleneck now. All that capital doesn't mean instant magic.

stories.title: The "Last Mile" Problem: Why Most AI Pilots Never Scale
stories.summary: Harvard Business Review analysis shows companies have hundreds of pilots but can't move beyond them—the bottleneck isn't tech or data, it's organizational redesign and change management.
stories.takeaway: Everyone's running AI pilots but only 1/3 of orgs are actually scaling past that. The problem? You can't just bolt AI onto old workflows—you gotta redesign the whole thing.

stories.title: Multimodal AI Agents Win the Customer Service Land Grab in 2026
stories.summary: CB Insights data shows voice, text, image, and video agents are where the real value is locking in—customer service orgs deploying multimodal agents are leaving competitors in the dust.
stories.takeaway: Text-only agents are already mid. The companies scaling voice + multimodal support are gonna own customer service. Voice is the proving ground that actually works.

watchNext.title: AI Brain Fry: The Mental Fatigue From Excessive Agent Use
watchNext.why: HBR study shows users hitting cognitive overload watching AI agents move too fast to comprehend. Lowkey important if your org is ramping up AI—burnout is real.

watchNext.title: China's OpenClaw AI Adoption Explodes; Tech Giants Racing to Deploy
watchNext.why: Tencent, ByteDance, JD.com all going all-in on an open-source agent framework. Shows the geopolitical split in AI adoption strategies + how fast things can move outside US.
